text: A full South Africa province revenue choropleth map is in the companion ebook (Libstar_Data_Story.docx, Figure 6). This sheet gives the same province-level detail in tabular/chart form for interactive filtering.

text: Pipeline: gzipped raw CSV -> Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2 -> Azure Data Factory mapping data flow (price/date/unit/boolean normalisation, brand lookup, dedupe) -> curated parquet -> Synapse serverless SQL views. Every rejected row carries an explicit reject_reason, not a silent drop.

title: Quarantined rows by reject reason
subtitle: Rows rejected by the ADF cleaning pipeline, by reason
footnote: bad_price / bad_date / unknown_brand / duplicate - see Data Quality sheet

title: Reconciliation vs Excel report / ebook
subtitle: Same KPI figures as the Excel report and ebook, for reconciliation
footnote: Source: rpt.vw_kpi_summary - single source of truth for all three deliverables

text: What 'anomaly' means: an IsolationForest model scored 1,000,000 products on price, cost, margin and weight together, and flagged the 2,000 whose combination is most unlike the rest of the catalog -- e.g. a heavyweight item priced like a small pack, or a product with an implausible margin. These usually signal a data-entry or pricing error, not genuine premium products. Recommendation: route the table below to category managers for manual review before next price-list publication; correcting these before they reach retail shelves protects margin and avoids customer-facing pricing errors.

title: Product segments (KMeans)
subtitle: Price vs margin, coloured by customer segment
footnote: Sampled for render performance - see ML Insights notes for full methodology

subtitle: Products where price deviates most from the category median
